project_privacy_data_flows.description: User enters username and password to log in to Wikipedia. Credentials sent to MediaWiki API via clientlogin action with login token for CSRF protection. Supports OATH two-factor auth and email authentication.

flow_steps.code: private fun doLogin() { val username = getText(binding.loginUsernameText); val password = getText(binding.loginPasswordInput); val twoFactorCode = getText(binding.login2faText) }
flow_steps.details: User taps login button after entering credentials. Username, password, and optional 2FA code collected from EditText fields.

flow_steps.code: fun login(coroutineScope: CoroutineScope, wiki: WikiSite, userName: String, password: String, ...)
flow_steps.details: LoginClient receives raw credentials. Gets login token from API then posts credentials via postLogIn.
Acquires login token from MediaWiki API for CSRF protection before sending credentials.

flow_steps.code: @FormUrlEncoded @POST(MW_API_PREFIX + "action=clientlogin&rememberMe=") suspend fun postLogIn(@Field("username") user: String?, @Field("password") pass: String?, @Field("OATHToken") twoFactorCode: String?, ...)
flow_steps.details: Credentials transmitted to api.php?action=clientlogin. Includes username, password, optional OATH 2FA token, captcha response, and login token. Response returns user ID and groups.

flow_steps.code: if (loginResult.pass() && userName.isNotEmpty()) { ServiceFactory.get(wiki).getUserInfo().query?.userInfo?.let { loginResult.userId = it.id; loginResult.groups = it.groups() } }
flow_steps.details: On success, retrieves user ID and permission groups from MediaWiki API. Updates local AccountManager account.
After successful login, triggers reading list sync, notification polling setup, and FCM push subscription update.

project_privacy_data_flows.description: Wikipedia uses Android AccountManager to store account credentials persistently. Background re-login uses stored credentials with rate limiting (max once per day).

flow_steps.code: object AccountUtil { ... fun updateAccount(response: AccountAuthenticatorResponse?, result: LoginResult) }
flow_steps.details: Account credentials managed via Android AccountManager with AccountAuthenticatorService. Supports automatic token refresh.

flow_steps.code: suspend fun loginBlocking(wiki: WikiSite, userName: String, password: String, ...): LoginResult
flow_steps.details: Background re-login with stored credentials. Rate-limited to prevent more than one attempt per 1-day period.
Android AccountAuthenticator service manages persistent account credentials.
